title: lithocarpus curtisii
intro: Lithocarpus curtisii, also known as the Borneo tanbark oak, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Borneo. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Lithocarpus curtisii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
